title: IBM Support Innovation
summary: Internal tools, prototypes, and support automations built across z/OS, Docker, and OpenShift support at IBM.
description: This is the enterprise capital in Phil's map. It captures the internal automation, rapid prototyping, support engineering, and customer-facing work that made up a major part of his professional career at IBM.
The public portfolio cannot expose the internal code directly, but it can represent the outcomes: automation, outage support, pre-sales prototypes, and multiple corporate awards for impact.
Today this city also represents Phil's current role as an AI Engineer for IBM z/OS, making it the practical and professional capital that anchors the rest of the world.

media.alt: A role-focused dashboard mockup representing IBM z/OS AI engineering work.
media.caption: A role-and-operations view representing enterprise automation, support, and AI systems work.

media.alt: An industrial walled city representing IBM support innovation work.
media.caption: A heavily fortified city built on automation, reliability, and enterprise support.

greatWorks.title: Automation Citadel
greatWorks.summary: Operational tooling and codified support knowledge that turned difficult systems work into repeatable leverage.

relationships.label: Operational rigor carried into public projects

highlights: Worked directly with enterprise and government clients during outage scenarios.
Led support innovation focused on codifying and automating resolutions.
Built Python and JavaScript tools that earned multiple IBM awards for impact.
productionQueue: Keep carrying enterprise reliability lessons into public-facing products.
Translate internal operational thinking into reusable personal systems.
